fields.description:
## Why
The editor round-trip was only tested via Python ElementTree (permissive) and never against the real bpmn-io libraries or a browser — so faithfulness/runtime bugs shipped undetected. Build E2E tiers that exercise the actual code the editor runs.

## What
A faithful, self-skipping E2E test infrastructure for the workflow visual editor, plus the editor view/edit remediation it surfaced.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [x] A Node-based serialization E2E tier drives the REAL bpmn-moddle (the editor's read/write layer) — tests/e2e/test_editor_e2e.py + tests/e2e/js/roundtrip.mjs.
- [x] A real-browser tier runs the actual bundle in headless Chromium (Playwright) and asserts edges render, no shape overlaps, the properties panel reacts to selection, an edit persists to the IR on Save, and morph preserves config — tests/e2e/test_editor_browser.py + tests/e2e/js/browser_*.mjs.
- [x] Both tiers self-skip when Node/Playwright/Chromium are absent, so the default Python suite is unaffected (registered 'e2e' marker; tests/e2e/conftest.py).
- [x] The faithful tier reproduced the branch-arm Save defect (illegal '@' NCName ids) and now passes after the fix.
- [x] Full workflow + e2e suite green.

## Verification
tests/e2e/ exists with the two tiers and conftest fixtures; pyproject registers the 'e2e' marker; commits 19d3985d, a2be0572, 2c66b1c7, 438cb0a5.
